nodes.name: 鹳形目
nodes.summary: 鹳形目（Ciconiiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 黑頸鸛
nodes.summary: 黑頸鸛（Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如黑頸鸛。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 鹳科
nodes.summary: 鹳科（Ciconiidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 黑頸鸛屬
nodes.summary: 黑頸鸛屬（Ephippiorhynchus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联2种鸟类，如凹嘴鸛、黑頸鸛。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: Ashburton River (Western Australia)
nodes.summary: The Ashburton River is located within the Pilbara region of Western Australia.

nodes.name: 黑頸鸛
nodes.summary: 黑頸鸛（Black-necked stork，学名 Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus），分类上属于鹳形目，并归入鹳科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联2个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、污染、猎捕压力等4项。
nodes.shortSummary: 黑頸鸛（學名：Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus）是鸛科中一種頸長腿長的涉禽。這種留鳥分佈於南亞次大陸、東南亞及澳洲（孤立族群）。牠們棲息於濕地環境，常在稻田或麥田等農地覓食各類動物獵物。成鳥兩性皆具厚重鳥喙，體色為白色帶虹彩光澤的黑色斑紋，但雌雄可透過虹膜顏…

nodes.name: Onslow, Western Australia
nodes.summary: Onslow, Western Australia是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑頸鸛，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=2019 |title=Confirmed nesting of Black-necked Stork Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus in lowland Nepal |journal=BirdingASIA |volume=31 |pages=88–90}} It extends into Southeast Asia, through New Guinea and into the northern half of Australia. Compared to other large waterbirds like cranes, spoonbills and other species of storks, black-necked storks are least abundant in locations that have a high diversity of large waterbird species. The largest population of this species occurs in Australia, where it is found from the Ashburton River, near Onslow, Western Australia, across northern Australia to north-east New South Wales

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=2019 |title=Confirmed nesting of Black-necked Stork Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus in lowland Nepal |journal=BirdingASIA |volume=31 |pages=88–90}} It extends into Southeast Asia, through New Guinea and into the northern half of Australia. Compared to other large waterbirds like cranes, spoonbills and other species of storks, black-necked storks are least abundant in locations that have a high diversity of large waterbird species. The largest population of this species occurs in Australia, where it is found from the Ashburton River, near Onslow, Western Australia, across northern Australia to north-east New South Wales

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It lives in wetland habitats and near fields of certain crops such as rice and wheat where it forages for a wide range of animal prey

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: |first7=Gopi |date=2017 |title=Records of Black-necked Stork Ephippiorhynchus asiaticus from the coastal areas of the Kutch district of Gujarat, India |journal=Forktail |volume=33|pages=135–137}} In cultivated areas, they prefer natural wetlands to forage in, though flooded rice paddies are preferentially used during the monsoon, likely due to excessive flooding of lakes and ponds. Bird nests are usually on trees located in secluded parts of large marshes or in cultivated fields as in India and lowland Nepal

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: They are threatened by habitat destruction, the draining of shallow wetlands, disturbance at nests, overfishing, pollution, collision with electricity wires and hunting.{{cite journal|last=Clancy, G.P
